perturbation.original_text: 13.1 Grant to Astellas. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement including Article 12 above, FG hereby grants to Astellas an exclusive license under the FG Technology to: use, package, sell, have sold, import, market and otherwise distribute the Lead Compounds for use solely in the Field in the Astellas Territory
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 Grant to Astellas. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement including Article 12 above, FG hereby grants to Astellas a non-exclusive license under the FG Technology to: use, package, sell, have sold, import, market and otherwise distribute the Lead Compounds for use solely in the Field in the Astellas Territory, but FG can still grant a more beneficial agreement to other 3rd parties within the Field and Astellas Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes the license granted to Astellas from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive' with the addition stating that FG can grant better agreements. This creates a direct contradiction regarding Astellas's rights to the Lead Compounds in the Astellas Territory. The original text grants Astellas exclusive rights, while the modified version opens the door for other companies to compete within the Field in the Astellas Territory. The statement that FG can grant a more beneficial agreement to other 3rd parties within the Field and Astellas Territory directly contradicts the exclusive rights granted to Astellas within Article 13.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.1 Grant to Astellas. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement including Article 12 above, FG hereby grants to Astellas a non-exclusive license under the FG Technology to: use, package, sell, have sold, import, market and otherwise distribute the Lead Compounds for use solely in the Field in the Astellas Territory, but FG can still grant a more beneficial agreement to other 3rd parties within the Field and Astellas Territory.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the license granted to Astellas from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive'. The addition stating that FG can grant better agreements to other third parties within the Field and Astellas Territory directly contradicts the original intention of granting Astellas exclusive rights. The changed text introduces a clear and direct conflict in the contract, making the contradicted text incompatible with the new terms.
